title: Did Family Leave Policies Stall Gender Pay Equity?
subtitle: 家事假政策是否阻碍了薪酬平等

paragraphs.en: Women have historically been paid less. But in the US in the 1980s, they began to catch up — fast. During that decade, the gender pay gap closed by about one percentage point a year. Had that trend continued, the gender wage gap would have been closed by 2017. But the trend didn't continue, and the gap remains yawning.
paragraphs.zh: 历史上女性薪酬一直较低。但在20世纪80年代的美国，她们开始迅速追赶。那十年间，性别薪酬差距每年缩小约一个百分点。若该趋势持续，到2017年性别薪酬差距本应消失。但趋势并未延续，差距依然悬殊。

words.meaning: adj. 巨大的，裂开大口的

paragraphs.en: According to a new study from academics at Harvard, the stagnation can be put down, perhaps counterintuitively, to the introduction of state and federal family leave policies. The academics argue that during the 1990s, as governments began to introduce leave policies, it was mainly women who took advantage of them. Though the policies might have helped those women to stay in the workplace, those who returned saw their wages had increased at lower rates than the men.
paragraphs.zh: 根据哈佛大学学者的一项新研究，薪酬差距的停滞或许有悖直觉地归因于州和联邦家事假政策的出台。学者们认为，20世纪90年代政府开始推行休假政策时，主要是女性利用了这些政策。尽管政策可能帮助这些女性留在职场而非因生育退出，但重返岗位者发现其薪资涨幅低于男性。

paragraphs.en: After family leave was introduced in the US, in fact, the rate of gender wage convergence fell to just 0.03 percentage points per year, and has remained there ever since. Those monitoring the process towards salary equity at work have long watched as progress slowed in many countries around the world. In fact, that progress began to reverse during the pandemic.
paragraphs.zh: 事实上，美国引入家事假后，性别薪酬趋同速度降至每年仅0.03个百分点，并自此维持该水平。关注职场薪酬公平进程的人士早已目睹全球许多国家进展放缓。事实上，疫情大流行期间这一进展开始逆转。

paragraphs.en: The gender pay gap is one of the most outstanding examples of that lack of parity, and still exists just about everywhere. The motherhood penalty has become a shorthand for describing why: In many places, especially rich countries, women earn the same as men until they reach their childbearing years. Women who have children begin to see their salaries slip behind their male counterparts.
paragraphs.zh: 性别薪酬差距是这种不平等最突出的例证之一，且几乎无处不在。“母职惩罚”已成为解释原因的简略说法：在许多地方，尤其是富裕国家，女性在育龄前与男性薪酬相当，有了孩子后薪资便开始落后于男性同行。

paragraphs.en: Part of this is because women take on more of the unpaid labor at home, which can eat into time available for work and energy for career advancement. But it's also because mothers are passed over for raises and promotion, and because time out of the workplace sets women back, even if that time is taken voluntarily, and supported by company or government policy.
paragraphs.zh: 部分原因是女性承担了更多家庭无偿劳动，挤占了可用于工作的时间和职业发展的精力。但也是因为母亲们在加薪和晋升时被忽略，且离开职场的时间让女性处于劣势，即便这段时间是自愿休取并得到公司或政府政策支持。

paragraphs.en: What would have happened if leave policies hadn't been introduced? The study doesn't go into that question, other than to say that if the 1980s trend continued, we would have been at parity by now. It's possible, however, that the journey towards wage parity would have stalled either way. If women's gains in the 1980s were made through the erasure of bias, once those problems were addressed, there would still be an issue with women taking breaks due to biological factors like breastfeeding.
paragraphs.zh: 若从未出台休假政策会怎样？该研究未深入探讨，仅指出若20世纪80年代趋势持续，如今早已实现平等。然而，无论哪种情况，通往薪酬平等的进程都有可能陷入停滞。如果女性在80年代的进步源于消除偏见，那么即便这些问题得以解决，因母乳喂养等生物学因素而需要休假仍是女性面临的难题。
